mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in stronger economic momentum.
failure_condition: If RSAFS rises month-over-month and the S&P 500 still posts negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of such instances over a 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from elevated levels signals a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as the market anticipates higher corporate profits.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls by 0.2pp or more in a month but the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than half of such events over 5 years, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is near zero and housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously rise, it indicates that accommodative monetary policy is successfully stimulating residential investment, a leading indicator of economic growth that pushes equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and HOUST has risen for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If the joint condition (low rates + rising housing starts) occurs but the median 20-day S&P 500 return is indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10) or negative, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During the early-cycle expansion phase, rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a broad-based recovery in manufacturing and investment, which is positively correlated with future S&P 500 returns as earnings growth accelerates.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO momentum (3-month change) and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) during early-cycle periods (identified by PCA of leading indicators), this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a modest threshold forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and lowering equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 3-month growth exceeds 2.5% and the S&P 500 still posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of such events over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above a moderate trend rate signals a robust economy with expanding corporate profits, prompting investors to bid up stock prices over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: If GDP growth above 2% annualized is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 40% of observations over a 10-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) continues to grow month-over-month while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains exceptionally low, it signals a synchronously strengthening labor market and still-accommodative policy, a powerful combination that lifts equity prices in the short term.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change positive and FEDFUNDS <= 0.5%
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but the weekly (5-day) S&P 500 forward return is negative in more than 60% of occurrences over a 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) acts as an early warning signal of economic contraction, leading investors to reduce equity exposure and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% in one month
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines >0.5% in a month but the S&P 500 5-day forward return is positive in more than half of such events over a 3-year roll-in, the hypothesis is falsified.
